decision: Electron Workbench first; project bundles, Markdown source dossiers, minimal truth/evidence status, hybrid scoped retrieval, explicit local/cloud execution policy, privacy-aware ActionTrace receipts, provider boundaries, and Git-compatible writes are foundational. Truth/citation/verification/execution package contracts are incubating; public knowledge packs, advanced local assistance, DSL, and canvas follow later.

kernels.name: source-core
must_not_own: canonical knowledge hidden only in JSON

kernels.name: context-core
owns: optional local semantic retrieval and reranking adapters

kernels.name: execution-core
kernels.mvp_note: Types may begin in shared-types and a small trusted service beside ai-core. Create a package after retrieval, AI, transcription, autocomplete, or verification provide multiple real consumers and tests.

kernels.name: ai-core
owns: execution policy and operation budget consumption

kernels.name: truth-core
kernels.mvp_note: Types may begin in shared-types and ai/context/source cores. Create a package only after multiple real consumers and tests exist.

kernels.name: citation-core
must_not_own: deciding whether a source is correct

kernels.name: verification-core
kernels.mvp_note: May begin inside ai-core/context-core behind a provider-neutral interface.

kernels.name: knowledge-core
must_not_own: provider-specific delivery as the only format

kernels.name: graph-core
must_not_own: anonymous edges without claim provenance when evidence matters

dependency_rules: Electron adapters are platform layers; core kernels should be testable without Electron when possible.
Project-core owns durable project membership; workspace-core owns UI layout and temporary inspection state.
Source adapters expose selections, anchors, provenance, and explicit imports but do not mutate notes directly.
Provider-grounded results do not bypass source-core to become durable knowledge.
Source knowledge lives in source.md dossiers whenever possible; sidecars support precision/performance only.
Material unsupported/disputed/stale status cannot exist only in a hidden sidecar.
Context-core assembles inspectable context packets from project files and installed packs before AI calls.
Retrieval relevance is not treated as epistemic support.
AI core returns response bundles and patch proposals; vault applies accepted patches.
Truth/citation validators never call AI.
Verification-core may call deterministic tools or provider adapters but never writes the vault directly.
Provider credentials and billing context remain behind trusted boundaries and never enter renderer or remote views.
Git-core is optional and must not become canonical storage or factual proof.
Docs update with boundary, file format, source dossier, claim/evidence, provider, knowledge-pack, context, and Git changes.
Context-core uses explicit, lexical, link, and structural retrieval before optional semantic stages by default.
Embeddings, rerankers, vector indexes, and model caches are optional rebuildable state.
Execution-core policy and budgets are enforced below renderer state.
Every meaningful retrieval/model/tool/verification/patch action can emit a privacy-aware ActionTrace.
Provider usage is represented as child ActionTrace data rather than a separate provider-only architecture.
Raw prompts, note bodies, transcripts, retrieved excerpts, and outputs are not captured in telemetry by default.
Local execution may report zero external billing but must not be represented as zero operational cost.
file_rules: Files are the durable source of record; evidence determines epistemic support.
Raw source assets are preserved beside Markdown dossiers.
Important claims may use human-readable claim/verification notes or optional adjacent precision sidecars.
Knowledge-pack source manifests and attribution are durable; pack indexes/embeddings are rebuildable.
Provider-grounded raw result collections are not committed as a reusable knowledge database.
Caches, indexes, embeddings, thumbnails, and provider caches are rebuildable or disposable.
Opening Atomik must not rewrite files unnecessarily.
One accepted AI operation or truth repair should produce one meaningful diff.
Private ActionTrace ledgers, local model files, and runtime caches are operational state and ignored by Git by default.
Reviewed aggregate usage reports may be exported explicitly without raw content.
